reviews.review_description:
I really enjoyed Squingle, it makes great use of VR. If you're into Puzzle games, and like the look of it, get it! 

As any good Puzzle game does, It starts off easy enough, and ramps up. I had to replay a few levels towards the end, improving my score to unlock the last two campaign worlds, as it gets pretty tough. But if you do get really stuck, the game can offer hints on where to go. I also found that a change of perspective can usually help work out those paths.

You can customise a lot in this game, including the size of the puzzles, which is a great feature if you're using a smaller play space. I preferred to play on maximum size in mixed reality, and move the puzzle around to fit my space, as I progressed through the various checkpoints. 

Bhaptics support is also well done, providing feedback when you're close to, or taking damage.  This reminded me a lot of the boardgame "Operation".  Put your kids in this setup for further entertainment :)

After completing the main game. There are (currently) two additional worlds that unlock, each with even more puzzles to complete. So lots of content.  I've got nothing negative to say, highly recommended!

developer_response.body: Great review and thanks so much!

reviews.review_description: So quality thank you

developer_response.body: Thanks RunningPenguin8382! Glad you like it :P

reviews.review_description: Trippy unique puzzle game. The graphics are even better than the videos show, you can set it for mixed reality with cool effects on your walls, or full vr that teleports you to a different dimension. The controls are really smooth and precise. I like the vibe this game has with the psychedelic puzzle god guiding you on the journey. Cant wait to see what other games this dev outs out

developer_response.body: "I like the vibe this game has with the psychedelic puzzle god guiding you on the journey." - thanks so much! I'll be working on updates for Squingle for a while before moving to the next game :P so keep your eyes peeled :D

reviews.review_description: Follow me for discount code will check on daily also!! Or follow link either one 👍🏼 so the is a Very cool very challenging quick tho!! Relaxing puzzle games!! Great job guys but maybe make a shaky hand setting caus buddy if someone had that due to a med condition would b nice and one up on every game I’ve seen so just saying. But beautiful and way to go 😍👍🏼

I met Ben briefly at breaking convention and had the immense pleasure of trying Squingle. I have to say it planted a seed in my mind and nearly a year later I’ve finally managed to get around to buying a quest 2, and of course squingle was my first software purchase. I’ve been having so much fun on it and am blown away by everything it offers! So much thought has clearly gone into every aspect from aesthetics to game mechanics to the options and settings that can drastically alter the play experience. 


Thank you Ben for creating something so fun and beautiful! There needs to be more games with such a chilled out, beautiful and psychedelic vibe!!

developer_response.body: "I am blown away by everything [Squingle] offers!" - thanks CRWK for the kind words! I might be at Breaking Convention again this year - see you there!

reviews.review_description:
Ok, so I read the reviews and heard the hype and couldn't imagine what this game was until I finally played it, and oh man, let me tell you, they were not over exaggerating. This thing is really cool!
So I have been looking for games that tax my mind and hand/eye coordination, etc. as I suffered a stroke during the pandemic, and had to do all of my verbal and physical rehab by myself. I actually did much of it in VR through the use of different apps. (Let me tell ya...trying for headshots only on zombies can be very rehabilitating,lol.) Although I was fortunate enough to be able to come back pretty well, there's still some hiccups every so often that I get thinking maybe I need to start doing some word searches, or crosswords, or something to tax the ol' noggin' lol. 
So this game is absolutely perfect to add to my little rehab arsenal because the thought you have to put into this to figure out the timing of the rotations and the size of the passageways and how quickly you can get through it, etc. really has you concentrating.
If you need to get your mind off of something, and feel like challenging yourself, this will do the trick. 
Another thing that's nice about it is it also serves as a calming bit of stress relief as well. And I'll be honest the music is awesome, but unfortunately, it doesn't always lend itself to the most calming and stress-free moments as you're trying to race the clock and get through to the goal. So what I did was turn the music off, play some soft ambient tunes and not worry about the time so I could just really enjoy the process of working through this beautiful environment, as well as when I have it sitting in my own room in front of me. ￼I found I really preferred not trying to better my times but rather just gently breathe and enjoy being in the moment. It's Zen at its best.
So look guys, if you want something to kinda take you away from things for a bit, especially if you don't have loads of time to go on crusades to hunt for treasure or shoot zombies, you can do some of these pretty quickly, and then move onto the next one, and go as far as your time allows and come back to continue on to the next later!
Give it a go folks, you'll have a great time!

reviews.review_title: Very fun puzzle title in MR
reviews.review_description:
This puzzle game is super simple to learn, and very hard to master. The premise is simple, In mixed reality there is a maze of sorts in front of you, using the trigger you grab a sphere or the center of spheres and try to guide them through a translucent maze to the goal without hitting the sides. This idea is simple and the game does a really good job starting you off with basic levels to guide you. They are easy but do not let this fool you, as you play more mechanics and objects are slowly introduced to ramp up the difficulty and keep you engaged in gameplay. 
    The art style and sounds in this game are amazing. You can play in mixed reality, but still see plenty of trippy beautiful designs and artwork.  The guide, a giant head that talks to you is also helpful and calming. This is a puzzle game and the same for any puzzle game of this type can be applied, you get lost in this by focusing on trying to figure out how to smoothly complete each maze and maximize your points.  I find this game incredibly relaxing and would definitely recommend it to anyone looking for a relaxing puzzle game that has a lot of challenging elements to it

developer_response.body: "Incredibly relaxing" - Thank you Sandwichx for taking the time for such a nice review. Sending love!

reviews.review_description:
When I'm exhausted from shooting zombies or using light sabers I really need a different pace in gaming.  Squingle scratches that itch.  If you like puzzling challenges this is a *must* have.

Its beautifully presented and such a unique experience.  No VR nausea and the mixed reality absolutely gives it the 'wow' factor. Just buy it already!

reviews.review_title: Chill vr experience, but no puzzles
reviews.review_description:
It’s a well-made experience to zone out and waste time, but as a huge fan of 3d puzzles, I couldn’t find a single one in Squingle. It’s more a test of dexterity if you try speedrunning levels. It’s similar to the game Operation, but relaxing instead of anxiety inducing. 😂 Not a bad experience, but I expect puzzles to require some sort of thought to complete or do well, even if only minimally. 

Mechanics are interesting, but implemented quite slowly and never present any difficulty leaving you doing lots of busy work while you wait for the puzzle to start. I didn’t 100% the game, but after over 50 levels, I gave up on ever seeing a puzzle. I was bored to tears the whole time. (Yes I got the three petals crown thing on all the levels I did do. It was very easy despite me having hand tremors, so don’t worry if you have poor coordination due to ill health. You can still play just fine.)

I wish they used the foundation to make actual difficult or more interesting mazes. The axial spin shift was particularly promising, but alas, I was just left bored and doing obvious tasks and busy work. 

Very chill zone-out and turn off your brain app though. Probably not for me. Perhaps if you are a small child or very very very bad at spatial reasoning it could be interesting.

Edit to respond to devs: 
Squingle certainly does not “balance difficulty” in any way and I was in no way satisfied since there is literally zero difficulty. Last I checked I was a type of player. I am not an expert anything and I absolutely did not have an “incredible time”. I acknowledge that I may be in the minority, but you cannot gaslight me into liking your game. That’s insane. You’re just making baseless assertions directly contrary to my entire experience without listening to genuine feedback. 

And as for your claim that the game starts to become interesting in the last 20%, even if that is true, it is absolutely not worth the time waste of the first part being pointless mind-numbing busy work. Anyone who is interested in using their brain will have died of boredom by the time you get to the part that could actually draw people in. Allegedly. Again, I can see my opinion is not universal but for the life of me I cannot see how. I did not insult you or say it’s a badly made app. I tried to point out its good points. But you cannot pretend that it’s some interesting puzzler when it lacks any sort of challenge or forethought. The disregard and dishonesty is even more off-putting than the original experience, but you’ve made it clear that I am not the intended audience. A shame, since I actually love 3d puzzles. Good day.

developer_response.body: Squingle balances difficulty to satisfy all types of player. You'll find reading others' reviews that many specifically call out and praise the level of challenge and the level design. I'm certain you will find challenge in the next 50 levels, especially the last 20 levels! And then there is 2x speed mode, which will test you even more! So, even expert players can be challenged and have an incredible time with Squingle. I reckon it's a lot like tetris in that way. And, you can always join the Discord and give your thoughts and ideas for what you'd like to see in the future, we have a lot planned!

reviews.review_description:
Squingle is a unique VR experience that challenges your patience and coordination while enveloping you in a beautiful and phantasmagorical audio visual display.   There is nothing anything like it in my extensive library of Quest games.  I have gotten hours of enjoyment out of Squingle.

I am very happy to see this wonderful experience is now on the main store.  Hopefully it will get the exposure it deserves and find a large audience.

developer_response.body: "Hours of enjoyment" ~ Thanks so much for the kind words! It’s awesome to hear Squingle stands out in your library and that you’ve had a great time with it. We’re really glad to be on the main store now, and fingers crossed it reaches even more players. Appreciate the support!

reviews.review_title: Great way to zone out and relax!
reviews.review_description: This game is a great way to relax and zone out into a psychedelic state while getting through levels. I love the use of the mixed reality mode which makes your house look trippy and makes you feel like a hippy. Combine the hand tracking mode and bhaptics support and you can fully relax while getting a massage. Hand tracking works great! Can't wait to see more added to this game!

Great fun! I love playing in MR. Also I watched my anime while playing that was a definite plus. Lots of replayability to get full stars and green dots I left a lot of both behind trying to get as far as I could this session. A new take for the VR puzzle genre as far as I know, it's like playing Operation almost with rhythm and flow. Like most puzzle games some way to allow players to make puzzles would probably increase the longevity and interest of this game.

Squingle is a super enjoyable meditative puzzle experience, similar to the children's game buzz wire.
The game is really fun and introduces more and more mechanics as you get deeper into it, keeping the game interesting at all times.

Squingle can be played in MR or fully immersive modes, and also has hand-tracking support.

For those who love a challenge, there is also a leaderboard where you can compete to get the fastest times.

Overall it's just a really nice tactile puzzle game that I enjoyed throughout, and for those who need a bit of stress-relief it is quite relaxing play as well.

Squingle is an absolutely mesmerizing VR puzzle game that offers a unique blend of challenge and charm. The surreal visuals draw you in, creating an immersive experience that feels both captivating and otherworldly. Guiding the revolving orbs through intricate 3D mazes is not only clever but also incredibly addictive, making it hard to put down. The progressive soundtrack enhances the journey, perfectly complementing the game's atmosphere.

What sets Squingle apart is its squidgy logic, which invites players to think outside the box while providing a satisfying sense of accomplishment with each solved puzzle. Competing with friends on the leaderboards adds an exciting layer of competitiveness, pushing you to improve your skills and race against your own ghost. The Mixed Reality update makes the experience even more personal and engaging.

With the highest user rating in the Quest store, it's clear that Squingle’s full game has resonated with players. The clever design and enchanting gameplay create a perfect balance of challenge and enjoyment. If you're looking for a VR/MR game that combines puzzle-solving with a dreamy aesthetic, Squingle is a must-play! If you want to sample the game first, check out Squingle Mini.

reviews.review_title: Excellent puzzle game, great dev support
reviews.review_description:
An excellent relaxing puzzler to encourage a steady hand, awareness and good timing. There's more to it than you first think, but the features introduction and learning curve is perfect. The visuals, audio, controls and various options are all great. The mixed reality mode shines.

I purchased this nearly 2 years ago when it first appeared on App Lab (now called legacy edition), if you did the same the dev will give you a free code for this new version. Great support, great game!

Squingle is a bit like a wire loop game, but on steroids. 

It starts out quite easy and difficulty ramps nicely, I'll definitely be aiming to complete this game.

It's loads of fun for anyone that enjoys puzzlers.

I highly recommend it!

What a nice surprise this game is!  The calm, soothing atmosphere makes it an ideal game after a hard day at work! The gameplay is simple yet precise, and completing the levels gives a very satisfying feeling (I love driving the orbs, which are linked together, each in a different spiral passage!) The tunnels through which the orbs must pass seem somehow organic, and the effect is perfectly rendered in both VR and MR. Both versions (VR or MR) are a visual delight, and for my part, I alternate between the two. I really enjoyed the game, and hope to see other games of this kind that operate perfectly with the particularities of the MR and VR experience.

There are no puzzles to see here. This is a game where you have to move 2 rotating balls through a maze without touching the walls. I think this is a great use case for hand tracking and mixed reality. The level design is a bit tedious, and I find the repeated voiceover obnoxious. (Hopefully this gets fixed.) Regardless, I found the overall experience quite fun. There is also room for mastery with speedrunning if you are into that.

I loved the idea that you can switch between mixed and vr environments while playing this game. If you are a fan of puzzles and tetris like games, you will love this game. I highly recommend this title.

I absolutely love this game and all the puzzles it provides. The game can be challenging while still feeling simplistic, and the visuals are great too. I would love if there was a way to create your own puzzles and play puzzles created by other people too, but I can see how that would interfere with dlcs.

developer_response.body: "I absolutely love this game." ~ Thanks for the kind words! The idea of creating and sharing puzzles is a great one! Feel free to join our Discord and drop this suggestion in the feedback section. We’d love to hear more ideas like this and see what the community thinks. Thanks for playing!

reviews.review_description: This game is a joy to play. From the visuals, to game play this game is brilliant. Iv been having a blast working my way through each challenge.

The game is really fun but its something missing, i dont really know what but it just needs something more. Either way its fun and adicting and i love playing this when i feel bored. I rated this game a 4 star cus it is really fun but needs something more, now i dont know what its missing but its propably gonna be added later. Btw i am a play tester at impact labs.
Beautiful MR puzzler with 100 levels. The mechanics are introduced step by step and require both timing and level awareness.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for leaving a review! Glad you are having fun.

reviews.review_description: Had alot of fun playing. Definitely recommend to anyone. Look forward to seeing more levels
this game to me was about taking my time and being patient and moving through the levels while having so much fun! Great relaxation game!

This is an amazing and relaxing way to work your mind.  I will begin by saying how well the sounds and music flow  together especially with all the visuals in vr mode.  The wall effects were a nice little addition as well.  It added that extra little spark I didn’t know it was missing.  
  The gameplay was fun while the difficulty varied keeping you on your top game.  I enjoyed increasing the map sizes while standing to help with times on the few speed runs I attempted.  Otherwise it was relaxing to sit back in the couch and slowly work through the levels. With 100 levels there is plenty to check out or race for the leaderboards.  
  Lastly this game is perfect for those wanting to be fully in virtual reality or mixed.  Both modes were great and made playing enjoyable in different circumstances.  The hand tracking was pretty great as well.  Never really felt I need to switch to controllers unless going for quick times.

reviews.review_title: Excellent relaxing and very calming game
reviews.review_description:
What a unique relaxing game with soothing music and the gameplay is fluid.

It's kind of trippy and has a trance effect to it, especially when you add walls,the colours are absolutely amazing.
Even in MR it's amazing.

Game gets more difficult as you progress through it, which is expected, by then you have mastered how to rotate and move through the levels, as it's tutorial is great.

Definitely a must buy for a unique puzzle kind of game.

Well done to the developers,thank you.

developer_response.body: "Definitely a must buy" ~ thanks so much for the kind words! Please do spread the word :P

reviews.review_description:
There's not much to describe, it's exactly what you'd expect from the trailer, but executes it very well. It's a peaceful and calming game with good atmosphere from the visuals and music. Puzzles are satisfying and fun, with the game's gimmick being pretty fun to work with at its core. Sometimes can be hard to parse depth and where exactly the walls are, but it's not enough to cause major issues, and I don't see how they could have improved this, so I won't dock a star for this.

DISCLAIMER: I received this game for free, for playtesting and review through Impact Labs. The developer was given no input or oversight on my review, nor was I given expectation of rating the game well.

This game was very calming to play. Solving puzzles in vr was certainly a new experience. Squingle ties together fun with a way to just sit down and relax. There are so many levels this game has to offer, leaving you with something to do on your down time.

Squingle is a fun and calm and unique game and it can be adictive to try to set the best time you can for the leaderbord but i only have a quest 2 so im not sure how much beter it whould be in mixed reality and if your unsure if you want to get it i advice first trying squingle mini to see if you like it

developer_response.body: "Fun and calm" ~ Glad you’re enjoying the game! Squingle plays great on the Quest 2, but mixed reality definitely adds a unique layer—especially with color passthrough, which brings a new level of immersion. Trying Squingle Mini first is a great tip for anyone unsure. Appreciate you giving it a go!

reviews.review_description:
My initial thoughts on the game, were that it reminded me of that carnival game, where you have a ring around some sort of obstacle course, and you try to get through it without touching that obstacle course. This is like that, but you're holding a ball, and the obstacles are around the ball.
It's obviously considerably more complex than that though! And I honestly really like it!
This feels like one of those games that's really only possible IN VR, since the difficulty comes from moving an object through 3D space, as well as understanding that 3D space.
Despite how complex I'm making it sound, it's honestly really simple! The game does an amazing job at explaining it all to you!

Talking about the visuals for a second, I honestly REALLY like them! The game has a very 'weird' feel to it, in a good way. They might seem like they'd be distracting, but they're surprisingly not! I actually think they HELP me concentrate!

Honestly a really solid game! If you think you'd like it, then I suggest trying it out!

This has to be one of the most relaxing games I’ve played in a long while. 

Love the zen atmosphere and the fact you can just zone out and do some puzzles, defo a new go to game for me.

I believed Content Creators when they said they couldn’t see why this was for them - glad I thought to give it a try anyways. This is relaxing, mesmerizing, well thought out and not overly complex but challenging enough to keep you entertained. SO worth picking up!

This game is sneaky because it starts you off with very basic levels which are actually just the tutorial. Once the full mechanics of the game are revealed, the timer and the way you can stretch the edges a little makes more sense.

With multiple orbs, it also really makes you think about things more when moving through the 3D space.

The music is relaxing and the graphics are trippy. There's a slightly unsettling feeling that you're removing something from an alien's intestine as you trace along the paths but that's just part of the weird charm of it.

Although you're working against the clock, it's still a relaxing and kind of meditative game.

If you like puzzle games, this is definitely worth considering.

I am so glad I took a chance on Squingle. This has quickly become one of my favorite ways to relax. The visuals are stunning and I love being able to play in mixed reality too. Definitely check this one out.

developer_response.body: "One of my favorite ways to relax" - Thanks StormyCs for your kind words about Squingle! Please do let me know what you'd like to see in future updates to Squingle :P

reviews.review_description:
I had some good fun playing this, and it's because I love games that build upon the initial mechanics to make it more difficult.

I won't spoil anything past the first new mechanic, but they make simple levels more complex by adding a new element: you gain an orb and they start spinning. It started off as guiding one orb through a maze, and then it became two spinning in the same direction. It's creative and a blast.

It's definitely worth getting if you enjoy those kinds of games, because each set of levels adds something new that makes you want to keep going until you unlock the next, and the cycle continues.

Props to the devs for making such a creative game! And you have the option to play in MR or VR, whichever you prefer!

developer_response.body: "Such a creative game!"—Appreciate the feedback! Glad to hear you enjoyed how the mechanics build on each other—it’s something we put a lot of thought into it,  so it’s cool to see that stood out for you. Thanks for giving it a go and sharing your thoughts!

reviews.review_description:
Game given to test by Impact Labs.

....and if not why not?!

Okay, a big disclaimer at the start:

*I love puzzle games!*

So yes, I write this review with some bias. But honestly can you blame me? 

As far as the puzzle genre goes, Squingle is top of its class! This is truly a game filled with brain teasing levels which will have you rubbing those temples as you try to figure out a way to get your Squingle to the finish line!

Everything about this game screams polish and passion from the developers. The music is relaxing and perfectly ambient, the narrator is well voiced and soothing, the visuals are wild, colorful and immersive and the puzzles are well designed & full of variety.

Replayability comes in 3 forms - collect all the orbs, finish the level with max petals and finally join the leaderboard with the fastest times. If you are like me then you will want to complete all three of those goals!!

There is one quality of life change I would make - allowing the player to keep the position of the puzzle across a level restart (useful when trying to get a fast score), but this is not an issue 99% of the time.

Squingle also comes with MR mode, a spatial wall effect mode and handtracking to suit the mood of any play style and visual interest.

I would love to see multiplayer and a level editor added at some point.

This truly is setting the bar high for puzzle game enthusiasts and VR experiences. 

I highly recommend trying Squingle Mini first if you are unsure about the game - I did and it got me hooked! ❤️

I'm a big fan of flat screen puzzle games already; adding VR to the mix with MR sets this above your typical puzzle game. The starter levels are well designed with adding more complex game play one step at a time until you are set loose on the more complex levels with layers of traps and complex level design. The game will have you going back to replay previous levels to get your best times and compete on the leaderboard and hone in your gameplay. Fantastic game! - I played the mini version and if you enjoyed that, you will love the full version, more levels, more complexity. One item I did not notice at first was the hand tracking function. Playing MR, with hand tracking was one of those "Wow, this is what VR games are meant to be like" moments, which after playing games for over 25 years are very fun to get.

This game is super cool! When you start you think hey this is super easy but then quickly as more elements are added it becomes much more challenging. You can play at your own pace and really get lost. I like the mixed reality option but my favourite is playing fully immersed in the VR environment. This game is high quality!

This is a fantastically made puzzle game. I found myself just kicking back in mixed reality and maneuvering my way through these puzzles. I enjoyed the progression of difficulty with adding additional obstacles or ways of moving. Each stage stayed fresh and interesting. Using hand tracking or controllers both worked flawlessly. I'll definitely be coming back to this game time and again any time I need a moment away from reality and a time to reflect.
